commented_code:
+package cmd
+
+import (
+	"archive/zip"
+	"fmt"
+	"io"
+	"os"
+	"path/filepath"
+	"strings"
+
+	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
+)
+
+func init() {
+	var exportConfigCmd = &cobra.Command{
+		Use:   "export-config [output-path]",
+		Short: "export Wave Terminal configuration",
+		Long:  "Export Wave Terminal configuration files into a zip archive",
+		RunE:  runExportConfig,
+	}
+	rootCmd.AddCommand(exportConfigCmd)
+}
+
+func runExportConfig(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
+	if len(args) != 1 {
+		return fmt.Errorf("export-config requires an output path")
+	}
+
+	outputPath := args[0]
+	if !strings.HasSuffix(outputPath, ".zip") {
+		outputPath += ".zip"
+	}
+
+	configDir := getWaveConfigDir()
+	if err := zipDir(configDir, outputPath); err != nil {
+		return fmt.Errorf("export-config failed: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	fmt.Printf("Configuration exported to %s
", outputPath)
+	return nil
+}

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -0,0 +1,88 @@
+package cmd
+
+import (
+	"archive/zip"
+	"fmt"
+	"io"
+	"os"
+	"path/filepath"
+	"strings"
+
+	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
+)
+
+func init() {
+	var exportConfigCmd = &cobra.Command{
+		Use:   "export-config [output-path]",
+		Short: "export Wave Terminal configuration",
+		Long:  "Export Wave Terminal configuration files into a zip archive",
+		RunE:  runExportConfig,
+	}
+	rootCmd.AddCommand(exportConfigCmd)
+}
+
+func runExportConfig(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
+	if len(args) != 1 {
+		return fmt.Errorf("export-config requires an output path")
+	}
+
+	outputPath := args[0]
+	if !strings.HasSuffix(outputPath, ".zip") {
+		outputPath += ".zip"
+	}
+
+	configDir := getWaveConfigDir()
+	if err := zipDir(configDir, outputPath); err != nil {
+		return fmt.Errorf("export-config failed: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	fmt.Printf("Configuration exported to %s\n", outputPath)
+	return nil
+}
discussion_comments.comment_body: @weilirs this shouldn't ever happen but it's probably good for posterity. Could you check that the config dir path is not empty before walking?

when extraFile is nil, this technical works and just returns an error (https://cs.opensource.google/go/go/+/refs/tags/go1.22.0:src/os/file_posix.go;l=19 -- does a nil check), but feels not so good.  add an `if` here and only call Close if extraFile is not nil.
can use resolvePosInt(newScreenIdxStr, 1).  the default case won't get used because of your check above.  this will also protect against 0 and negative numbers.

 	RunningCmds      map[base.CommandKey]*RunCmdType

discussion_comments.comment_body: sudoPw should be a `[]byte` not a `*[]byte`.  it is a slice, so it is still possible to set it to nil.

bug fix - I found a bug where if I ran `/copyfile ~/'` there would be a nil ptr dereference because metacmd parsing fails and newPk here ends up as nil. rtnErr isn't properly checked here, so I fixed this by adding a return if there is an err here
GetRpcContext is never nil, the type doesn't allow it
